title: buildah run/bud --isolation=chroot $c <cmd> fails with single uidmap for unprivileged user
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
buildah run/bud **fails with chroot isolation** if user is not configured in subuid/subgid map files. It also ***fails with oci isolation and crun runtime**. However **succeeds with rootless isolation with crun runtime**. Is this expected ? 

Fail: isolation=chroot
Fail: isolation=oci runtime=crun
Success: isolation=rootless runtime=crun

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. remove user entries from /etc/subuid /etc/subgid
2. c=$(buildah from debian:10-slim)
3. buildah run --isolation=chroot $c true 

**Describe the results you received:**
```
buildah run --isolation=chroot $c true
error clearing supplemental groups list: operation not permittedsubprocess exited with status 1
                                                                                               exit status 1
```

```
buildah run --isolation=oci --runtime=crun $c true
2020-07-12T03:34:45.000749013Z: mount `devpts` to '/dev/pts': Invalid argument
error running container: error creating container for [/bin/true]: : exit status 1
error while running runtime: exit status 1
ERRO exit status 1
```

```
buildah run --isolation=rootless --runtime=crun $c true
echo $?
0
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
 buildah run --isolation=chroot $c true
echo $?
0 

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.15.0-1.fc32.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```

Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev

CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
image Version:   5.5.1
Git Commit:

```

[storage.options]

```

runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

rootless_storage_path = "$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
[storage.options.vfs]
ignore_chown_errors = "true"
```

comments.body: @rhatdan could you please comment on this. Thanks
These are working for me with the overlay driver?  Why are you using the VFS Driver?
Very interesting.  I don't know why. @giuseppe @nalind Any ideas?
I got similar errors with Overlay.

the error from the OCI runtime:
```
2020-07-14T20:42:01.000174777Z: mount `devpts` to '/dev/pts': Invalid argument
```

happens because buildah specifies gid=5 in the mount options, but there is no gid=5 with a single mapping.  Podman handles this case and drops the gid=5 if there are not enough mappings.


The other issue with setgroups happens because the namespace was configured with a single mapping writing directly to `/proc/$PID/*id_map` and for doing that it is necessary to set `deny` into `/proc/$PID/setgroups`

PR here: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2468

The issue with the runtime instead should be solved using `--isolation=rootless`

@giuseppe @rhatdan 

Will this enable podman build to run with single uid mapping and chroot isolation without need of runc or crun?

> Will this enable podman build to run with single uid mapping and chroot isolation without need of runc or crun?

yes, you should be able to use podman build without an OCI runtime
I think we can close the issue since the PR was merged
@rhatdan can we get a minor release with this fix?
I think making a 1.15.1 would involve cherry-picking the desired fix onto `release-1.15` and cutting the release from the branch.

If we have no new features, then we can do this. Also do we want these fixes in RHEL8.3?  
If there are new features we should cut 1.16.0 And RHEL8.3.1 will get the new version.
